steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends: "greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use." The word "alone" is the key signal — proximity is ruled out as the sole explanation. The correct answer must capture the point the text actually makes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It could be due to inaccuracies in the survey results.’: the passage treats the survey data as valid and draws conclusions from it — survey inaccuracies are never raised. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It was much larger than the researchers conducting the study expected.’: the passage never mentions researcher expectations or surprise. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents.’: matches the passage exactly. Mexico City residents live farther from parks yet use them more, so proximity alone can't explain the gap — something else must. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It was calculated using sources that predate the survey.’: no mention of pre-existing sources anywhere in the passage. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
